notes: Primary key. decimal(10,0) carries no fractional part and fits within INT64 range. Uniqueness enforced after every load per acceptance criteria.

Partition column with DAY granularity. Enables efficient date-range pruning for HR analytics queries and supports the 60-day retention policy management.
Cluster column. Low-to-medium cardinality job identifier co-located with department_id and status to improve filter performance on analytical queries.
NUMERIC preserves the two decimal places of precision required for monetary values without floating-point rounding errors.
Nullable in source; NULL values preserved. NUMERIC retains the two decimal place precision of the source decimal(4,2).
Self-referential FK to employee_id. Nullable for top-level employees who have no manager. decimal(10,0) has no fractional part and fits INT64.
Cluster column. Low cardinality department identifier as the first cluster key provides the greatest pruning benefit for department-filtered analytical queries. Nullable in source.
Cluster column. Very low cardinality (e.g. active or inactive) placed third in the cluster key to support status-filtered analytical and reporting queries.
Record creation timestamp from the source system. NOT NULL in source; null constraint validated pre-load.
Nullable. Primary high-watermark column for daily incremental load strategy: WHERE updated_date > last_load_watermark OR (updated_date IS NULL AND created_date > last_load_watermark).
